semantic.role: GitLab CI template for automated architecture compliance checking using ArchSpine
responsibilities: Automated execution of `spine sync` and `spine check` in the CI pipeline
Posting violation comments to merge requests via GitLab API
Configuration instructions for required CI variables and prerequisites
outOfScope: Detailed explanation of `archspine` subcommands or output
ArchSpine installation outside of CI contexts
Support for CI platforms other than GitLab CI

English.purpose: To provide a reusable GitLab CI template that enforces ArchSpine architecture rules during merge request workflows.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers and DevOps engineers using GitLab CI who wish to integrate ArchSpine's architecture check into their pipeline. It assumes the repository has already been initialized with `spine init` and that the required CI variables (LLM_API_KEY, GITLAB_TOKEN) are available.
key_takeaways: Include this template in `.gitlab-ci.yml` to run architecture checks on every merge request event.
Set CI variables `LLM_API_KEY` (for LLM calls) and `GITLAB_TOKEN` (for MR comments) beforehand.
If `spine check` detects violations, a detailed comment is automatically posted on the MR, and the pipeline fails.
The job runs only on `merge_request_event` triggers, saving resources.
On failure, an artifact (`spine-check-output.txt`) is retained for 7 days for troubleshooting.
